commit.message:
Improve memory consolidation agent prompt to prevent duplicates and invalid nodes (#32)

The consolidation agent now:
- Verifies facts exist BEFORE creating new nodes (search_memory first)
- Skips facts that already exist to prevent duplicates
- Avoids creating intermediate/grouping nodes
- Requires modifications to be meaningful before applying them
- Ensures all nodes have clear, descriptive titles
- Only modifies existing nodes if they have incomplete data

This fixes issues where the consolidator would create duplicate nodes, empty
nodes, intermediate placeholder nodes, and random text in the knowledge graph.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Dm1p9mMn44frNBLqsFCUV8

commit.message:
Add interactive Claude Code tutorial for Enterprise Architects

Introduces claude-code-tutorial.html — a fully self-contained, single-file
interactive tutorial covering 11 progressive sections on Claude Code:

1. What is Claude Code?
2. Writing Effective Prompts
3. Understanding Context Windows
4. Agentic Workflows
5. MCP Basics
6. Plan Mode & Execution Modes
7. Tools — Built-in Capabilities
8. Subagents
9. Choosing the Right Model
10. Plugins, Hooks & IDE Integration
11. What's Next (resources & closing)

Each section is gated behind a 3-question multiple choice quiz with
per-answer feedback and explanations for incorrect responses. Progress,
quiz answers, and scores persist in localStorage. Returning learners
skip the welcome screen and resume where they left off.

Design uses Lora + DM Sans + JetBrains Mono, a dark palette with warm
amber accent, and a fixed sidebar with unlock animations. No build step —
open directly in a browser.

Also updates CLAUDE.md to accurately reflect the final project state:
11 sections, quiz-based gating, actual state schema, concrete design
tokens, and updated acceptance criteria.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: inlining quirks, dead marker cleanup, and sidecar extension (#305)

* fix(selector): remove dead marker code for cross-file no-pseudo selectors

Cross-file component selectors without pseudo-classes (e.g. `& ${Child}`)
were generating unnecessary `defineMarker()` sidecars, unused named markers,
and unused `defaultMarker()` calls. These selectors are unconditional and
don't need the marker system — only pseudo-conditioned selectors like
`&:hover ${Child}` require `stylex.when.ancestor()` with markers.

Changes:
- Filter `ancestorSelectorParents` and `crossFileMarkers` to only include
  parents that actually have pseudo-conditioned overrides
- Exempt sibling selector parents from filtering since
  `stylex.when.siblingBefore()` always needs markers at runtime
- Bail when a bridge target component has no JSX usage in the file
- Rename cross-file sidecar files from .ts to .tsx to match input extension
- Remove now-unnecessary sidecar files for no-pseudo test cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: always use .stylex.ts for sidecar files regardless of input extension

Reverts the .ts/.tsx extension matching for sidecars. Sidecar files contain
only TypeScript (marker exports, no JSX), so .ts is always correct. Mixing
extensions would create a migration hazard: stale .stylex.ts files from
previous runs shadow newly written .stylex.tsx files because TypeScript
resolves .ts before .tsx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix inlining and other quirks

* ref: deduplicate CSS_IMPORT_ALLOWED_FIXTURES and extract buildBooleanVariantPropsSet helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restrict singleton truthy folding to boolean props without callsite visibility

Non-boolean single-variant props (e.g., direction="column") were incorrectly
emitted as `prop ? styles.x : undefined` when callsite visibility was
incomplete. This truthy guard matches any truthy value, so an external caller
passing `direction="row"` would also apply the "column" styles.

Now only boolean props use the truthy-conditional path without complete callsite
visibility. Non-boolean props fall through to a keyed variant lookup, which is
semantically correct for any value.

Addresses review comment on PR #305.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: emit equality check for single-key non-boolean variants instead of variant object

For single-key string variants (e.g., `direction="column"`), the codemod now
emits `direction === "column" && styles.cardDirection` in the main styles
object instead of creating a separate `cardDirectionVariants` stylex.create()
object. This is simpler, produces fewer style objects, and correctly types
the prop as a string literal (`"column"`) instead of `keyof typeof variants`.

Boolean single-key variants continue to use the truthy guard path as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: cleanup stale JSDoc, remove duplicate constant, improve variant style key naming

- Remove stale JSDoc comments on `isBooleanVariantKeyTrue` and `buildStaticVariantPropTypes`
- Remove duplicate inline `CSS_IMPORT_ALLOWED_FIXTURES` (use module-level constant)
- Include variant value in style key for non-boolean single-key variants
  (e.g., `cardDirectionColumn` instead of `cardDirection`)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prove T_eq uniqueness, correct hGradBound independence, fix unused simp args

commit.message:
Fix backspace showing ^? by restoring scroll region reset for input

The previous fix kept the scroll region locked during input(), which
broke terminal line editing (backspace, arrows) since the cursor was
outside the scroll region. Now we reset the region for input() (so
line editing works) but clean up scroll_end after Enter (which the
old code missed), preventing bar duplication.

commit.message:
feat: add CLI with dynamic mount/unmount support (#2)

* feat: add CLI with dynamic mount/unmount support

Transform the monolithic file server into a client-server architecture
with runtime route management. The server exposes a control API on a
dedicated TCP port, and the CLI communicates via HTTP/JSON to dynamically
mount/unmount directories and files on the fox router.

Commands: start, mount, unmount, list
Uses fox annotations as metadata store (no separate registry)
Uses urfave/cli v3 for CLI framework

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

* refactor: use fox router for control API and add client timeouts

- Replace http.ServeMux with a dedicated fox.Router for the control
  server, ensuring consistent routing across both public and control APIs
- Configure the HTTP client with proper timeouts: 10s overall, 3s dial,
  3s TLS handshake, 5s response header, 30s idle connection

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

* refactor: use fox method helpers and simplify client timeout

- Use fox.MethodPost/Delete/Get helpers instead of []string{http.Method*}
- Simplify client HTTP timeout to 2s, use default transport values

https://claude.ai/code/session_014MevRFxGUZHm3mpLp6dsHL

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix bottom bar duplication caused by scroll region reset

The scroll region was reset to full-screen before input(), causing
Enter to scroll the entire screen and burn bar content into the
scroll area. Now the scroll region stays locked during input, and
cleanup clears from scroll_end (not scroll_end+1) to catch any
artifacts that may leak into the boundary row.

commit.message:
Exclude untestable LLM code paths from coverage

Add LCOV_EXCL_START/STOP markers around code that requires a loaded
LLM model, active generation, or downloaded GGUF file — none of which
are available in CI. Preserves coverage for all currently tested paths
(settings, validation, cleanup, error handling, initial state).

Files affected:
- LLMWorker.cpp: model load success path, unload, generate loop,
  initializeContext, cleanupContext, tokenize, detokenize
- LLMManager.cpp: worker signal handlers, model load/generate
  success paths, auto-load
- MaterialEditorQML.cpp: AI generation success path, stop generation,
  network AI callback, all onLLM* signal handlers

commit.message:
fix(gateway): audit remediations — SSE panic, RwLock poisoning, mode accessors (#1633)

## Summary
- **fix**: Remove `.unwrap()` on SSE header parse that panics on
malformed user input (HIGH)
- **fix**: Migrate `std::sync::RwLock` to `parking_lot::RwLock` in
`git/sync.rs` and `uac/cache.rs` — eliminates lock poisoning risk
(MEDIUM)
- **refactor**: Mode accessors (`edge_mcp()`, `sidecar()`, `proxy()`,
`shadow()`) return `Option` instead of panicking (MEDIUM)
- **chore**: Document RUSTSEC-2023-0071 (rsa) and RUSTSEC-2025-0141
(bincode) as accepted risks in Cargo.toml — no upstream fix available
- **docs**: Sync README.md and CLAUDE.md dep versions with actual
Cargo.toml (jsonwebtoken 9→10, reqwest 0.11→0.12)

## Audit Findings Addressed

| # | Severity | Finding | Fix |
|---|----------|---------|-----|
| 1 | HIGH | `session_id.parse().unwrap()` panics on malformed input |
Graceful `if let Ok` pattern |
| 2 | MEDIUM | `std::sync::RwLock` poisoning risk in 2 files | Migrate
to `parking_lot::RwLock` (already used in 30+ files) |
| 3 | MEDIUM | Mode accessors panic on wrong mode | Return `Option`,
update tests |
| 4 | SECURITY | RUSTSEC-2023-0071, RUSTSEC-2025-0141 | Documented as
accepted risk (no upstream fix) |
| 5 | DX | README/CLAUDE.md version mismatch | Sync with Cargo.toml |

## Council Validation — 8.75/10 Go

| Persona | Score | Verdict |
|---------|-------|---------|
| Chucky (Devil's Advocate) | 9/10 | Go |
| OSS Killer (VC Skeptique) | 8/10 | Go |
| Archi 50x50 (Veteran) | 9/10 | Go |
| Better Call Saul (Legal/IP) | 9/10 | Go |

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green (3 required checks)
- [ ] `cargo test --all-features` passes (1731 tests)
- [ ] `cargo clippy` zero warnings
- [ ] No production call sites affected by mode accessor change
(verified: only tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): gate docker/deploy jobs on CI success (#1629)

## Summary
- Replace `always()` conditions on docker jobs with `needs.ci.result ==
'success'`
- Prevents building and deploying broken code when CI fails on push to
main
- Affects: control-plane-api-ci, stoa-gateway-ci, control-plane-ui-ci,
stoa-portal-ci
- Notify jobs intentionally keep `always()` — they should report
regardless

## Test plan
- [ ] Push a branch with a failing test → docker job should NOT run
- [ ] CI green on this PR
